spec_match.rationale: Per CLAUDE.md's 'Removals / deprecations' guidance: probe for the removed feature + show the recommended replacement. This page does both — probes XSLTProcessor constructor and live-transform, then renders the same data via DOMParser + template literal.

interactive.rationale: Two probes (constructor + live transform), an editable XML textarea, render button, and live re-render on input. Hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'probes whether XSLTProcessor still exists and still runs in this browser, then shows a working DOMParser+template literal replacement' — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Catches the 'ctor present but transformToFragment broken' middle state — good. Doesn't probe `<?xml-stylesheet?>` processing instruction loading (which is a separate code path from XSLTProcessor and has its own removal timing), nor the response-MIME-type path.

references.rationale: See-also links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/4709671889534976 and the WHATWG removal issue.

design_system.rationale: Most colours from variables, but inline style on .article markup uses raw style attributes (acceptable, not raw hex). The error path uses `color:var(--accent-rose)` inline — fine. No major violations.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

openQuestions.title: Add a probe for `<?xml-stylesheet?>` processing instruction
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus blurb mentions both XSLTProcessor and the `<?xml-stylesheet?>` PI as separate removal paths. The page only probes the JS constructor. Add a third probe that loads an XML document with `<?xml-stylesheet href='x.xsl' type='application/xslt+xml'?>` via fetch + DOMParser and checks whether Chrome applies it — that's the path that breaks for legacy RSS / sitemap pages with embedded XSLT links.

openQuestions.title: Surface real-world breakage examples
openQuestions.detail: The 'who will this break' question is the most important one for visitors. Surface a list of canonical XSLT-using sites and stylesheets (the chromestatus motivation cites several) with a 'check live' button per row that fetches and probes. Even a static curated list would help a lot.

openQuestions.title: Compare DOMParser+template vs Mustache vs lit-html
openQuestions.detail: The 'replacement' panel is one specific style of templating. A toggle that switches between the inline template-literal approach, a Mustache-like spec, and a lit-html-style tagged template would show that the replacement is a choice of templating library, not a single thing.

openQuestions.title: Show the removal-timeline + Chrome version readout
openQuestions.detail: Display the Chrome version, the chromestatus removal milestone (143), and the current version's stage in the deprecation timeline (deprecated → enterprise reverse-trial → removed). Helps visitors understand 'I am on Chrome N — what should I see here'.

summary: Good probe + replacement structure following CLAUDE.md's removal/deprecation pattern. The biggest gap is the missing `<?xml-stylesheet?>` PI probe — that's the path that breaks for the RSS/sitemap pages most commonly cited as casualties of the removal.
